title: go-cli command specification
description: Declarative specification of a cobra command hierarchy consumed by the go-cli library.

issue-url.description: URL users are directed to for filing bugs, propagated to every command in the hierarchy.

app-id.description: Identifier used to scope the application's on-disk storage (config, cache, data, runtime). A string applies to every host. A mapping selects the identifier per host operating system, falling back to its `default` entry, then to the root command's `name`, then to the running binary's name.

update-sources.description: Per-source configuration for update checking, keyed by source name. Each key must match a provider registered with cli.UpdateProvider, and its value is decoded into that provider. The active source is selected by cli.BuildSource.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration decoded into the provider registered under this name.

appIDByHost.description: Identifiers keyed by host operating system, so each host can use the form its conventions call for.

default.description: Identifier used on any host without its own entry.

windows.description: Identifier used on Windows, conventionally an application name such as `ExampleCLI`.

macos.description: Identifier used on macOS, conventionally a reverse-DNS bundle identifier such as `com.example.foo`.

linux.description: Identifier used on Linux, conventionally a lowercase system name such as `example-cli`.

name.description: Name the command is invoked by. A runner is bound to it by its id path: the dot-delimited names of the command and its ancestors, e.g. "app.remote.add". The usage synopsis is generated from the arguments the bound runner registers.

aliases.description: Alternative names the command can be invoked by.

examples.description: Example invocations shown in help output.

summary.description: Short, one-line description of the command.

hidden.description: Whether the command is hidden from help output.

deprecated.description: Deprecation message shown when the command is used. A non-empty value marks the command deprecated.

groupCommands.description: Named groups of subcommands. The reserved group "default" holds commands that belong to no group; any other key names a titled group.
